stemHtml: When an aircraft subjected to an act of unlawful interference must depart from its assigned track or its assigned cruising level without being able to make radiotelephony contact with ATS, what should the pilot-in-command do, whenever possible?

options.a: Proceed in accordance with procedures that have been established and promulgated in the Regional Supplementary Procedures (Doc 7030).
options.b: Proceed at a level which differs from the cruising levels normally used for IFR flight by 150 m (500 ft) in an area where a vertical separation minimum of 600 m (2000 ft) is applied.
options.c: Proceed at a level which differs from the cruising levels normally used for IFR flight by 75 m (250 ft) in an area where a vertical separation minimum of 300 m (1000 ft) is applied.
